name: Adducted / Internally Rotated Shoulder
description: Shoulder held in adduction and internal rotation, arm pressed against trunk

name: Flexed Elbow
description: Elbow held in flexion, difficulty with extension

name: Pronated Forearm
description: Forearm held in pronation, difficulty supinating

name: Flexed Wrist
description: Wrist held in flexion, may include radial or ulnar deviation

name: Clenched Fist
description: Fingers flexed into palm, difficulty opening hand

name: Thumb-in-Palm
description: Thumb adducted and flexed into palm. Note: refractory cases may also need flexor pollicis brevis and opponens pollicis (not yet in this dataset).

name: Flexed Hip
description: Hip held in flexion, difficulty with extension during gait

name: Adducted Hip (Scissoring)
description: Hips adducted with scissoring gait pattern, legs crossing midline

name: Stiff Knee Gait
description: Knee held in extension during swing phase, difficulty with clearance. Rectus femoris is the primary target (it's biarticular and active in swing-phase knee flexion). Vastus intermedius may contribute as a secondary target. Avoid the other vasti — they stabilize the knee in stance phase and weakening them collapses weight-bearing.

name: Flexed Knee
description: Knee held in flexion, crouch gait pattern

name: Equinus (Plantarflexion)
description: Ankle held in plantarflexion, toe-walking pattern

name: Equinovarus
description: Ankle in plantarflexion and inversion, foot turned inward. Note: tibialis anterior is typically WEAK in this pattern (not spastic) — its weakness is a diagnostic feature; do not inject it.

name: Claw Toes / Curled Toes
description: Toes flexed/clawed, painful with shoe wear

name: Striatal Toe (Hitchhiker Toe)
description: Great toe held in extension/dorsiflexion

name: Cervical Dystonia / Torticollis
description: Abnormal head/neck positioning — rotation (torticollis), lateral flexion (laterocollis), extension (retrocollis), or flexion (anterocollis). Includes the major cervical dystonia targets: SCM, splenius capitis, semispinalis capitis, scalenes, longissimus capitis, upper trapezius, and levator scapulae.

name: Trunk Lateral Flexion (Pisa Syndrome)
description: Lateral trunk lean, pelvic hiking

name: Blepharospasm
description: Involuntary forceful eyelid closure. Injection targets the orbicularis oculi (pretarsal > preseptal), corrugator supercilii, and procerus.

name: Oromandibular Dystonia
description: Involuntary jaw movements — jaw closing (masseter, temporalis, medial pterygoid) or jaw opening (lateral pterygoid). Assess opening vs closing pattern before selecting targets.

name: Bruxism / Jaw Clenching
description: Involuntary or habitual jaw clenching and teeth grinding. Masseter is the primary target; temporalis is secondary.

name: Anterocollis (Forward Head)
description: Head/neck flexed forward. Targets include SCM (bilateral), scalenes, and platysma. Caution: bilateral SCM injection carries high dysphagia risk.

name: Retrocollis (Head Extension)
description: Head/neck extended backward. Primary targets: splenius capitis, semispinalis capitis, longissimus capitis, upper trapezius.

name: Laterocollis (Head Tilt)
description: Head tilted laterally toward the shoulder. Ipsilateral scalenes, levator scapulae, splenius capitis, and SCM (clavicular head) are common targets.

name: Trunk Flexion (Camptocormia)
description: Involuntary trunk flexion when standing/walking. Rectus abdominis and obliques are the primary targets. Note: some cases are treated by injecting the paraspinals if they are the dystonic muscles pulling the trunk forward via co-contraction.

name: Trunk Rotation
description: Involuntary trunk rotation / twisting. Obliques (contralateral external + ipsilateral internal) are the primary targets.

name: Trunk Extension (Axial Dystonia)
description: Involuntary trunk extension / backward arching. Paraspinal muscles (erector spinae group) are the primary targets. Inject at 2-3 vertebral levels.
